source_papers:
- '[[openalex-2607.08567-functional-dependence-and-synchronous-coupling-in-ergodic-au]]'
title: Lyapunov Exponents and Natural Representations
---

**Background:** In the study of ergodic Markov chains generated by iterated random maps, the natural innovation representation often serves as a basis for evaluating functional dependence measures. However, this choice of representation does not necessarily yield the expected memory loss properties, particularly when the Lyapunov exponent of the dynamical system is positive.

**Question / Future Work:** Determine whether a negative Lyapunov exponent is sufficient to guarantee that the natural innovation representation provides a valid Bernoulli shift representation with decaying functional dependence coefficients, beyond existing globally contractive frameworks.

**Why It Matters:** Understanding the limits of natural innovation representations is vital for the correct application of functional dependence measures, which are essential tools in modern nonlinear time series analysis.

**Evidence:** it would be of considerable interest to determine whether a negative Lyapunov exponent is sufficient to recover the natural Bernoulli shift representation beyond the globally contractive framework of [4] or the geometric moment contraction condition of [21].
